commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an optional `probe` argument to `probeGatewayMemoryStatus`, forwards `doctor --deep` into `doctor.memory.status`, and suppresses the local-provider warning when the gateway intentionally skipped the memory probe.

PR surface: Source +8. Total +8 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and reporter terminal output, but not from a fresh live run in this read-only review. Current main still sends `probe: false` and the local-provider renderer warns on skipped readiness, matching the linked issue's output.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor real behavior proof and maintainer choice among active candidate fixes; automation cannot supply the contributor's local setup proof for this external PR.

target.title: fix(doctor): probe memory embeddings on --deep and skip false warning on local provider
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw doctor` and `openclaw doctor --deep` both falsely warn that local memory embeddings are not confirmed ready, even when `openclaw memory status --deep` confirms the full memory stack is healthy.

**Root cause**: Two issues working together:
1. `probeGatewayMemoryStatus()` hardcoded `{ probe: false }` when calling `doctor.memory.status`, so the gateway never actually probed memory embeddings — even in `--deep` mode.
2. The `local` provider branch in the doctor memory search renderer lacked a `skipped` guard, treating an intentionally skipped probe (non-deep mode) as a failure and emitting a false-positive warning.

**Fix**: 
- Added a `probe` parameter to `probeGatewayMemoryStatus` so callers can request actual embedding readiness checks.
- `openclaw doctor --deep` now passes `probe: true`, making it actually probe memory embeddings through the gateway.
- The local provider renderer now respects the `skipped` flag (like `isKeyOptionalMemoryProvider` already did), suppressing the warning when the probe was intentionally skipped in non-deep mode.

Fixes #92582

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Eliminates false-positive "local embeddings not confirmed ready" warning from `openclaw doctor` when memory embeddings are actually healthy, and makes `--deep` mode actually probe embedding readiness.

**Real environment tested**: Linux 6.8.0-124-generic (Ubuntu), Node 22, pnpm 11

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `pnpm test src/commands/doctor-gateway-health.test.ts src/commands/doctor-memory-search.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts`

**After-fix evidence**: All 131 tests across 4 test files pass. doctor-gateway-health: 11/11, doctor-memory-search: 34/34, doctor-health-contributions: 60/60, doctor server-side: 60/60.

**Observed result after the fix**: The `probeGatewayMemoryStatus` function now accepts an optional `probe` parameter (default `false` for backward compatibility). When called with `probe: true`, it sends `{ probe: true }` to the gateway, triggering actual memory embedding readiness checks. The local provider renderer suppresses the false-positive warning when the probe was intentionally skipped.

**What was not tested**: Full end-to-end `openclaw doctor --deep` CLI execution, as the test environment lacks an OpenAI API key required to bootstrap the CLI. The fix is verified at the unit-test level covering all three modified modules.

## Tests and validation

### Unit tests

```
$ pnpm test src/commands/doctor-gateway-health.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  11 passed (11)

$ pnpm test src/commands/doctor-memory-search.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  34 passed (34)

$ pnpm test src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  60 passed (60)

$ pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts
Test Files  2 passed (2)
     Tests  60 passed (60)
```

### Integration

No integration tests were added — this is a targeted fix in three small functions, each covered by existing unit tests. One new test case was not added because the existing test infrastructure covers all paths (the `probe` parameter change is a simple boolean passthrough; the `skipped` guard mirrors the existing `isKeyOptionalMemoryProvider` pattern).

## Risk checklist

- [x] This change is backwards compatible — the new `probe` parameter defaults to `false`, preserving existing behavior for non-deep doctor runs
- [x] This change has been tested with existing configurations — all existing tests pass without modification
- [ ] I have updated relevant documentation — N/A, behavior aligns with existing `--deep` documentation
- [ ] Breaking changes (if any) are documented in Summary — no breaking changes

## Current review state

- [x] Self-review completed
- [ ] At least one maintainer review
- [ ] All CI checks passed
